finding: `atm next` can recommend non-runnable status commands during active-work or broker coordination. In Plan 3.1 dogfood, `brokerStatusCommand` advertised `node atm.mjs team broker status --json`, but the CLI only supports `team broker resolve`, so copy-pasting the suggested command returns `ATM_CLI_USAGE`. The same guidance surface also advertised `node atm.mjs tasks status --json` even though `tasks status` requires `--task`.
expectedBehavior: Every statusCommand / brokerStatusCommand emitted in `next`, readiness hints, and first-layer compact orientation must be copy-paste runnable or explicitly marked as a template with required placeholders. Broker observability should route to the supported command surface, e.g. `node atm.mjs broker status --json`, and task status hints should include a concrete task id when one is known.
evidenceOrRepro: Plan 3.1 dogfood on 2026-07-22 after closing ATM-GOV-0249: `node atm.mjs next --prompt "Plan 3.1 broker resolution authority parity gap: prepare ATM-GOV-0255 for external captain claim" --json` emitted `brokerStatusCommand: node atm.mjs team broker status --json` and `statusCommand: node atm.mjs tasks status --json`. Running `node atm.mjs team broker status --json` returned `ATM_CLI_USAGE` with text `team broker supports: resolve`.
followUp: Audit all guidance-emitted statusCommand fields for runnable-command parity. Add regressions that execute the emitted active-work broker/status commands from `next` output and fail if any command returns usage-error.
